title: quercus glauca var. gracilis
intro: Quercus glauca var. gracilis, also known as Japanese blue oak, is a small evergreen tree or shrub native to East Asia, characterized by its glaucous leaves and slender branches. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Quercus glauca var. gracilis are attributed to its ability to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and possess potential anticancer properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and bark are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are consumed to leverage its medicinal properties.
